sections.subtitle: Match Overview
sections.text: Deportivo Alavés, currently in 17th place with 35 points, face off against Real Sociedad, who sit in 11th place with 43 points in La Liga Round 33 at the Estadio de Mendizorroza in Vitoria-Gasteiz. This matchup promises to be intriguing as Alavés fight to escape the relegation zone while Sociedad aim to climb higher in the mid-table. The referee for this game, Mateo Busquets Ferrer, averages 5.5 yellow cards per game, which could influence the physicality of this encounter. Both teams have had mixed results recently, setting the stage for a competitive clash.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head History
sections.text: In their last 5 matchups, Real Sociedad and Deportivo Alavés have each secured 2 wins, with 1 draw, showing a balanced rivalry. Their most recent encounter on August 28, 2024, saw Alavés triumph 2-1 away at Sociedad, with Sergio Gómez earning Player of the Match honors with a 7.6 Sofascore rating. Historically, 5 of the last 6 head-to-head games have had less than 2.5 goals, indicating tight, low-scoring affairs. Additionally, 5 of the last 7 matches saw both teams scoring, suggesting defenses often struggle to keep clean sheets in this fixture.

sections.subtitle: Deportivo Alavés Key Players and Stats
sections.text: Deportivo Alavés will rely heavily on forward Kike García, who has scored 12 goals this season against an expected goals (xG) value of 8.43, overperforming by 3.57. Midfielder Joan Jordán also stands out with a strong 7.07 average rating and 4 goals. Defender Nahuel Tenaglia, a standout in past matchups against Sociedad with a 7.4 average rating and 1 goal in 3 starts, could be crucial in defense. Alavés have scored 35 goals this season (15th in the league) but struggle with possession, averaging just 45.35% (16th rank). Their strength lies in penalty area entries per game, ranking 2nd with 30.79, and they convert big chances at a league-leading 45.28% rate.

sections.subtitle: Real Sociedad Key Players and Stats
sections.text: Real Sociedad boast talent like Sergio Gómez, who ranks 5th in the league for big chances created (14) and has a 7.13 average rating with 5 assists. Forward Mikel Oyarzabal, in form with 6 goals in his last 5 matches and an average rating of 7.6, will be a major threat. Midfielder Takefusa Kubo also impresses with 5 goals and a 7.07 rating. Defender Jon Martin stands out with an incredible 81.7% aerial duels won, the best in the league. Sociedad have shown attacking intent, with 11 of their goals coming in the final 15 minutes of matches, but they’ve gone 11 games without a clean sheet.

sections.subtitle: Tactical Insights and Team Struggles
sections.text: Deportivo Alavés commit the most fouls in the league (556, ranked 1st) and average 2.79 yellow cards per game (18th rank), which could test referee Mateo Busquets Ferrer’s patience. Their defensive solidity is evident with 7 clean sheets (11th rank) and strong tackling stats (19.12 tackles per game, 1st rank). However, they’ve struggled to score early, with only 15.22% of goals in the first 15 minutes. Real Sociedad, meanwhile, often start slowly with just 5.41% of goals in the opening 15 minutes but excel late, scoring 27.03% of their goals between the 46th and 60th minutes. Their streak of 6 out of 7 games with over 2.5 goals shows attacking flair but defensive vulnerability.

sections.subtitle: Missing Players Impact
sections.text: Deportivo Alavés will miss defender Abdelkabir Abqar, who ranks 5th in the league for percentage of long balls in total passes (9%), potentially impacting their defensive distribution. Real Sociedad face a bigger challenge with key forwards like Orri Steinn Óskarsson (3 goals) and Ander Barrenetxea (3 assists) absent, alongside defender Nayef Aguerd, who ranks 4th in ground duels won percentage (72%). These absences could hinder Sociedad’s attacking output and defensive stability, giving Alavés a slight edge in certain areas.

sections.subtitle: Interesting Facts and Streaks
sections.text: Deportivo Alavés have had 5 out of 6 recent home games with more than 4.5 cards, reflecting their physical style, especially with 95 yellow cards this season (18th rank). Real Sociedad have struggled defensively, failing to keep a clean sheet in their last 11 matches across all competitions. Head-to-head, Alavés have gone 8 games without a clean sheet against Sociedad at home, which might boost the visitors’ confidence. Both teams have seen less than 10.5 corners in 5 of their last 7 encounters, pointing to matches with limited set-piece action.
